name: Salt Water Taffy
recipeIngredient: 1-1/2 teaspoons vanilla or mint flavoring, or a few drops of oil of mint, rum, or brandy extract
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar, water, corn syrup, and salt in a 4-quart saucepan.
2. Stir until boiling, washing down any undissolved sugar from the sides of the pan with a pastry brush dipped in water.
3. Add the butter and continue boiling without stirring until the candy thermometer registers 256 to 264 degrees. The lower temperature will give a chewier taffy, and the higher heat a more brittle product.
4. Turn out the hot batch on a marble slab or large platter that has been oiled well with vegetable oil.
5. If using a large marble slab, turn out the hot batch into two pools.
6. Add a few drops of food coloring to the top of the candy (or add several colors while pulling).
7. When the edge of the taffy can be comfortably touched with well-buttered or oiled fingers, form it into a mass by turning inward.
8. Taffy is ready to pull as soon as it can be handled.
9. Grease palms and fingers well before handling.
10. Take the warm, flowing candy up with the fingertips and pull out about 12 inches at first.
11. Quickly turn the candy back from one hand to the other, then catch the center and again pull, always using the fingertips. This incorporates air in the mixture and gives its typical texture.
12. If adding food coloring during this step, pause briefly, put a drop or two of color along the taffy, and flop the mixture over on the color before continuing to pull until evenly mixed.
13. When the taffy is very hard to pull and holds its shape when laid out on the marble slab or pan, it is ready to break off into pieces (preferably cut with greased scissors).
14. If making several colors of taffy, pull each color until thin and then twist them together.
15. For a completely round candy like a peppermint stick or cane, roll the twisted mixture on the marble slab to make it even.
16. Store taffy in airtight containers such as cookie tins between sheets of wax paper or foil for any moisture in the air will make it quite sticky. Taffy is generally sold with pieces individually wrapped.
